impacts.statement: Water reaches the top of protection in South Frankfort.
Water reaches the top of protection in North Frankfort.
Areas outside of floodwall are covered. Many county roads are closed. Most of Buffalo Trace Distillery is flooded.
Buffalo Trace Distillery offices flood along Fair Oaks Lane.

Homes along Big Eddy Road flood. Parts of Port Collins Road and Polsgrove Road flood and cutoff homes. Water crosses Wilkinson Boulevard at Leestown Lane which cuts off Frankfort Housing Authority and Walter Todd Drive. Buffalo Trace Distillery Ash Building (across Wilkinson Boulevard) floods.
Bellepoint is cut off. Indian Gap Road, Bates Road and River Valley Road flood. Water Plant is affected. Todd Park floods.
Paul Sawyier Drive, East Second Street, East Fourth Street and Admirals Landing flood. The entrance at McDonald Ferry Road floods. Buffalo Trace Distillery Visitor Parking Lot floods.

Portions of Logan Street, Norton Street and Murray Street flood. Riverboat Landing is flooded. Steele Branch Road is flooded resulting in some homes being cut off and Kentucky River Campground closure.

KY 420 (Old Lawrenceburg Road) closes. Wilkinson Street (dead end), Watson Court, Wilson Street and Kentucky Avenue flood. Many basements of homes flood in south Frankfort along Paul Sawyier Drive and Hermitage Drive.
Taylor Avenue floods near quarry. Swallowfield Road floods at Bates Road.
Travis Circle, Onans Bend Road, and Polsgrove Road flood.
Steele Branch Road floods at dead end. Stoney Creek Road and Shadrick Ferry Road close. Buffalo Trace Distillery buildings along the river flood.
Benson Valley Road floods. Water approaches Stoney Creek Road.
Portions of Still Waters Campground flood.
Stoney Creek Road floods. Portions of Kentucky River Campground flood.

hydronotes.statement: River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 48 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

inundation.siteSpecificInfo:
The North Frankfort Levee System was designed to overtop at 51.5 ft and the South Frankfort Levee System was designed to overtop at 51 ft, assuming all sandbag lines, temporary protection and levee closure structures are in place.  The scenario presented on AHPS assumes that the levee/floodwall closure structures within the levee/floodwall system are in place; however, the temporary protection measures created by placing sandbag lines along the streets, outside of the flood protection system, are not assumed to be in place. Thus, floodwaters will flow around the levee system at a stage of 49.0 ft, unless the temporary protection measures are constructed.                                                                                                                                                    


In addition, flooding is not shown on the map for the W Clinton St. and Mero St. tunnels at the Capital Plaza, however, these tunnels may flood at river stages that equal or exceed 49 ft. In addition, the highway overpass at Wilkinson Blvd (421) and Holmes Street (2261) will be clear of floodwaters for all mapped elevations; the Holmes Street (2261) underpass will be flooded at river stages that equal or exceed 49 ft.
